0.title: About All Things Music
0.text:
About All Things Music
What is All Things Music?
All Things Music is a community-driven website that documents a wide variety of songs across many different genres. It provides a friendly experience that allows users to easily explore and discover a diverse range of music.
Our Goal
Here at All Things Music, our goals are to:
Create a welcoming collaborative environment
Document our community’s favourite songs
Share favourites within the the community
Introduce a more diverse musical experience to our users

Why we Do What we Do
We believe that by recording favourite songs within our community, our users will discover a far wider variety of music than they otherwise would.
Contributing
If you would like to add to All Things Music’s collection of songs, visit our GitHub repository and follow the listed contribution guidelines.

1.title: Blues Music
1.text:
Blues
The home base for all things blues music
Blues is a music genre originating in the Deep South of the United States, rooted in African-American work songs, spirituals, and folk music. Known for its soulful expression of hardship and resilience, the blues laid the foundation for rock, jazz, and R&B.
Little Red Rooster
Little Red Rooster by Howlin’ Wolf (1961)
A raw, slow-tempo Chicago blues classic featuring Howlin’ Wolf’s growling vocals and Willie Dixon’s iconic bass line. Later covered by The Rolling Stones.
The Thrill Is Gone
The Thrill Is Gone by B.B. King (1969)
One of B.B. King’s signature songs, blending blues with orchestral strings. A timeless expression of heartbreak and loss.
Cross Road Blues
Cross Road Blues by Robert Johnson (1936)
A legendary Delta blues recording that inspired generations of rock and blues musicians, famously associated with the myth of Johnson selling his soul at the crossroads.
Hoochie Coochie Man
Hoochie Coochie Man by Muddy Waters (1954)
Written by Willie Dixon, this Chicago blues anthem became one of Muddy Waters’ most iconic recordings, built on a powerful stop-time riff.

2.title: Classical Music
2.text:
Classical
The home base for all things classical music
Classical music is a broad tradition of Western art music spanning centuries, from the Baroque period to the modern era. Celebrated for its complexity, emotional depth, and technical mastery, classical music continues to inspire musicians and listeners worldwide.
Cello Suite No. 1
Cello Suite No. 1 in G Major by Johann Sebastian Bach (1717–1723)
One of the most beloved pieces in the cello repertoire, Bach’s first suite is elegant, introspective, and deeply moving — a cornerstone of Baroque music.
Moonlight Sonata
Piano Sonata No. 14 “Moonlight Sonata” by Ludwig van Beethoven (1801)
Beethoven’s hauntingly beautiful sonata, particularly its iconic first movement, remains one of the most recognisable pieces in all of classical music.
The Four Seasons
The Four Seasons by Antonio Vivaldi (1725)
A set of four violin concertos depicting the seasons of the year, Vivaldi’s masterpiece is a dazzling example of Baroque programme music.
Symphony No. 9
Symphony No. 9 in D Minor by Ludwig van Beethoven (1824)
Composed while Beethoven was completely deaf, this monumental symphony — featuring the iconic “Ode to Joy” finale — is considered one of the greatest musical achievements in history.

3.title: Electronic Music
3.text:
Electronic
The home base for all things electronic music
Electronic music encompasses a vast range of genres produced using synthesisers, drum machines, and digital audio workstations. From the underground clubs of Detroit and Chicago to global festival stages, electronic music has continually pushed the boundaries of sound.
Clarity
Clarity by Zedd feat. Foxes (2012)
A Grammy Award-winning electro-house anthem blending soaring vocals from Foxes with Zedd’s signature energetic production. A defining track of early 2010s dance music.
Around the World
Around the World by Daft Punk (1997)
A hypnotic house classic built on a single repeated lyric and an irresistible groove. One of the most iconic electronic tracks ever made.
Strobe
Strobe by deadmau5 (2009)
An epic progressive house journey that builds slowly over nine minutes into an emotional and euphoric climax. Widely regarded as deadmau5’s magnum opus.
Windowlicker
Windowlicker by Aphex Twin (1999)
A genre-defying electronic track combining IDM, acid house, and experimental elements. Aphex Twin’s twisted creativity on full display.

4.title: Folk Music
4.text:
Folk
The home base for all things folk music
Folk music is a genre rooted in the oral traditions of communities around the world, telling stories of everyday life, struggle, love, and history. From acoustic ballads to protest songs, folk music has always been the voice of the people.
Blowin’ in the Wind
Blowin’ in the Wind by Bob Dylan (1962)
One of Bob Dylan’s most celebrated songs, posing rhetorical questions about peace, freedom, and war. A defining anthem of the 1960s civil rights and anti-war movements.
The Sound of Silence
The Sound of Silence by Simon & Garfunkel (1964)
A haunting acoustic folk song exploring themes of isolation and failed communication. Later re-recorded with electric instrumentation, it became a massive commercial hit.
Both Sides Now
Both Sides Now by Joni Mitchell (1969)
A reflective and poetic folk classic from Joni Mitchell’s landmark album Clouds, exploring life’s illusions and the complexity of human experience.
Fast Car
Fast Car by Tracy Chapman (1988)
A deeply moving folk-pop song about escaping poverty and searching for a better life. One of the most powerful and enduring songs of the 1980s.

5.title: Hip-Hop Music
5.text:
Hip-Hop
The home base for all things hip-hop music
Hip-hop is a cultural movement and music genre that emerged in the South Bronx in the 1970s. Built on four pillars — DJing, MCing, breakdancing, and graffiti — hip-hop has grown into one of the most dominant and influential music genres in the world.
Parents Just Don’t Understand
Parents Just Don’t Understand by DJ Jazzy Jeff & The Fresh Prince (1988)
A Grammy Award-winning comedic hip-hop classic featuring Will Smith rapping about the relatable struggles of being a teenager. A cornerstone of old-school hip-hop.
Lose Yourself
Lose Yourself by Eminem (2002)
The Oscar-winning anthem from the film 8 Mile, capturing the intensity and determination of seizing a single opportunity. One of the greatest hip-hop songs ever recorded.
HUMBLE.
HUMBLE. by Kendrick Lamar (2017)
A Grammy Award-winning hip-hop track with a minimalist beat and razor-sharp lyricism. Kendrick’s confrontational style and cultural commentary made this an instant classic.
Empire State of Mind
Empire State of Mind by Jay-Z feat. Alicia Keys (2009)
A triumphant anthem celebrating New York City, blending Jay-Z’s confident lyricism with Alicia Keys’ soaring chorus. One of hip-hop’s most iconic tracks.

6.title: Jazz Music
6.text:
Jazz
The home base for all things jazz music
Jazz is an American music genre that originated in New Orleans in the late 19th and early 20th centuries. Characterised by improvisation, swing, and complex harmonies, jazz has branched into countless sub-genres including bebop, fusion, smooth jazz, and beyond.
Round Midnight
Round Midnight by Thelonious Monk (1944)
One of the most recorded jazz standards ever written, Monk’s moody and introspective composition perfectly captures the atmosphere of late-night jazz clubs.
So What
So What by Miles Davis (1959)
The opening track of Kind of Blue — the best-selling jazz album of all time — “So What” is a modal jazz masterpiece defined by its cool, relaxed groove.
Take Five
Take Five by The Dave Brubeck Quartet (1959)
One of the best-selling jazz singles of all time, written in an unusual 5/4 time signature. Paul Desmond’s alto saxophone melody is immediately recognisable worldwide.
A Love Supreme
A Love Supreme by John Coltrane (1964)
A four-part spiritual suite considered one of the greatest jazz recordings ever made. Coltrane’s deeply personal and transcendent performance elevated jazz to new artistic heights.

7.title: Pop Music
7.text:
Pop
The home base for all things pop music
Pop music is a genre characterised by catchy melodies, relatable lyrics, and broad commercial appeal. Constantly evolving, it draws influences from nearly every other genre and dominates charts around the world.
Blinding Lights
Blinding Lights by The Weeknd (2020)
A synth-pop masterpiece with 80s-inspired production that broke multiple chart records, spending more weeks in the Billboard Hot 100 top five than any song in history.
Shape of You
Shape of You by Ed Sheeran (2017)
A tropical pop hit that became one of the best-selling singles of all time, blending acoustic guitar with dancehall rhythms and an irresistibly catchy hook.
Bad Guy
Bad Guy by Billie Eilish (2019)
A darkly playful pop track from Billie Eilish’s debut album, produced by her brother FINNEAS. Its minimalist bass-driven sound and witty lyrics made it a global phenomenon.
As It Was
As It Was by Harry Styles (2022)
A melancholic yet upbeat synth-pop track that became one of the most streamed songs of 2022, reflecting themes of isolation and longing with an irresistible energy.

8.title: Rap Music
8.text:
Rap
The home base for all things rap music
Rap is a vocal style and music genre built on rhythm, rhyme, and storytelling. Originating as part of the hip-hop movement, rap has evolved into a global art form spanning countless regional styles, from East Coast lyricism to Southern trap and West Coast G-funk.
Miami
Miami by Will Smith (1998)
A fun, feel-good rap track celebrating Miami’s vibrant culture. Will Smith’s charismatic delivery and the infectious Latin-influenced beat made it a summer anthem.
Rap God
Rap God by Eminem (2013)
A technical tour de force showcasing Eminem’s jaw-dropping speed and lyrical dexterity. It set a Guinness World Record for the most words in a hit single.
m.A.A.d city
m.A.A.d city by Kendrick Lamar feat. MC Eiht (2012)
A vivid and intense portrait of life in Compton, California. Kendrick’s storytelling and the track’s dramatic beat switch make it one of the most gripping rap songs of the decade.
All Falls Down
All Falls Down by Kanye West feat. Syleena Johnson (2004)
A soulful and introspective rap track from Kanye’s debut album The College Dropout, critiquing consumerism and insecurity with heartfelt honesty.

9.title: Rock Music
9.text:
Rock
The home base for all things rock music
Rock music is a broad genre that emerged in the 1950s from rock and roll, blues, and country. Known for its electric guitar-driven sound, powerful rhythms, and energetic performances, rock has evolved into countless sub-genres including classic rock, punk, metal, and alternative.
Sweet Home Alabama
Sweet Home Alabama by Lynyrd Skynyrd (1974)
A Southern rock anthem with one of the most recognisable guitar riffs in music history. Originally written as a response to Neil Young’s criticism of the South, it became an enduring symbol of regional pride.
Bohemian Rhapsody
Bohemian Rhapsody by Queen (1975)
A six-minute operatic rock epic that defied every convention of the era. Freddie Mercury’s extraordinary composition blends ballad, opera, and hard rock into an unforgettable masterpiece.
Smells Like Teen Spirit
Smells Like Teen Spirit by Nirvana (1991)
The defining anthem of the grunge movement and Generation X. Kurt Cobain’s raw energy and the song’s explosive dynamics changed the direction of rock music forever.
Stairway to Heaven
Stairway to Heaven by Led Zeppelin (1971)
Widely regarded as one of the greatest rock songs ever written, this epic eight-minute track builds from a delicate acoustic intro to a thunderous climax, showcasing the band’s extraordinary musical range.
